metadata.title: High School — Statistics and Probability
domains: Conditional Probability and the Rules of Probability
metadata.notes: Auto-ingested from Common Standards Project (set 67810E9EF6944F9383DCC602A3484C23_D10003FB_high-school-statistics-and-probability). Node structure matches KnowledgeNode in knowledge_zome/integrity/src/lib.rs.

nodes.title: (+) Analyze decisions and strategies using probability concepts (e
nodes.description: (+) Analyze decisions and strategies using probability concepts (e.g., product testing, medical testing, pulling a hockey goalie at the end of a game).

academic_standards.description: (+) Analyze decisions and strategies using probability concepts (e.g., product testing, medical testing, pulling a hockey goalie at the end of a game).

nodes.title: (+) Use probabilities to make fair decisions (e
nodes.description: (+) Use probabilities to make fair decisions (e.g., drawing by lots, using a random number generator).

academic_standards.description: (+) Use probabilities to make fair decisions (e.g., drawing by lots, using a random number generator).

nodes.title: (+) Weigh the possible outcomes of a decision by assigning probabilities to p...
nodes.description: (+) Weigh the possible outcomes of a decision by assigning probabilities to payoff values and finding expected values.

academic_standards.description: (+) Weigh the possible outcomes of a decision by assigning probabilities to payoff values and finding expected values.

nodes.title: (+) Develop a probability distribution for a random variable defined for a sa...
nodes.description: (+) Develop a probability distribution for a random variable defined for a sample space in which probabilities are assigned empirically; find the expected value.

academic_standards.description: (+) Develop a probability distribution for a random variable defined for a sample space in which probabilities are assigned empirically; find the expected value.

nodes.title: (+) Develop a probability distribution for a random variable defined for a sa...
nodes.description: (+) Develop a probability distribution for a random variable defined for a sample space in which theoretical probabilities can be calculated; find the expected value.

academic_standards.description: (+) Develop a probability distribution for a random variable defined for a sample space in which theoretical probabilities can be calculated; find the expected value.

nodes.title: (+) Calculate the expected value of a random variable
nodes.description: (+) Calculate the expected value of a random variable; interpret it as the mean of the probability distribution.

academic_standards.description: (+) Calculate the expected value of a random variable; interpret it as the mean of the probability distribution.

nodes.title: (+) Define a random variable for a quantity of interest by assigning a numeri...
nodes.description: (+) Define a random variable for a quantity of interest by assigning a numerical value to each event in a sample space; graph the corresponding probability distribution using the same graphical displays as for data distributions.

academic_standards.description: (+) Define a random variable for a quantity of interest by assigning a numerical value to each event in a sample space; graph the corresponding probability distribution using the same graphical displays as for data distributions.

nodes.title: (+) Use permutations and combinations to compute probabilities of compound ev...
nodes.description: (+) Use permutations and combinations to compute probabilities of compound events and solve problems.
nodes.subdomain: Conditional Probability and the Rules of Probability

academic_standards.description: (+) Use permutations and combinations to compute probabilities of compound events and solve problems.

nodes.title: (+) Apply the general Multiplication Rule in a uniform probability model, P(A...
nodes.description: (+) Apply the general Multiplication Rule in a uniform probability model, P(A and B) = P(A)P(B|A) = P(B)P(A|B), and interpret the answer in terms of the model.
nodes.subdomain: Conditional Probability and the Rules of Probability

academic_standards.description: (+) Apply the general Multiplication Rule in a uniform probability model, P(A and B) = P(A)P(B|A) = P(B)P(A|B), and interpret the answer in terms of the model.

nodes.title: Apply the Addition Rule, P(A or B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A and B), and interpret ...
nodes.description: Apply the Addition Rule, P(A or B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A and B), and interpret the answer in terms of the model.
nodes.subdomain: Conditional Probability and the Rules of Probability

academic_standards.description: Apply the Addition Rule, P(A or B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A and B), and interpret the answer in terms of the model.

nodes.title: Find the conditional probability of A given B as the fraction of B's outcomes...
nodes.description: Find the conditional probability of A given B as the fraction of B's outcomes that also belong to A, and interpret the answer in terms of the model.
nodes.subdomain: Conditional Probability and the Rules of Probability

academic_standards.description: Find the conditional probability of A given B as the fraction of B's outcomes that also belong to A, and interpret the answer in terms of the model.

nodes.title: Recognize and explain the concepts of conditional probability and independenc...
nodes.description: Recognize and explain the concepts of conditional probability and independence in everyday language and everyday situations.
nodes.subdomain: Conditional Probability and the Rules of Probability

academic_standards.description: Recognize and explain the concepts of conditional probability and independence in everyday language and everyday situations.

nodes.title: Construct and interpret two-way frequency tables of data when two categories ...
nodes.description: Construct and interpret two-way frequency tables of data when two categories are associated with each object being classified. Use the two-way table as a sample space to decide if events are independent and to approximate conditional probabilities.
nodes.subdomain: Conditional Probability and the Rules of Probability

academic_standards.description: Construct and interpret two-way frequency tables of data when two categories are associated with each object being classified. Use the two-way table as a sample space to decide if events are independent and to approximate conditional probabilities.

nodes.title: Understand the conditional probability of A given B as P(A and B)/P(B), and i...
nodes.description: Understand the conditional probability of A given B as P(A and B)/P(B), and interpret independence of A and B as saying that the conditional probability of A given B is the same as the probability of A, and the conditional probability of B given A is the same as the probability of B.
nodes.subdomain: Conditional Probability and the Rules of Probability

academic_standards.description: Understand the conditional probability of A given B as P(A and B)/P(B), and interpret independence of A and B as saying that the conditional probability of A given B is the same as the probability of A, and the conditional probability of B given A is the same as the probability of B.

nodes.title: Understand that two events A and B are independent if the probability of A an...
nodes.description: Understand that two events A and B are independent if the probability of A and B occurring together is the product of their probabilities, and use this characterization to determine if they are independent.
nodes.subdomain: Conditional Probability and the Rules of Probability

academic_standards.description: Understand that two events A and B are independent if the probability of A and B occurring together is the product of their probabilities, and use this characterization to determine if they are independent.

nodes.title: Describe events as subsets of a sample space (the set of outcomes) using char...
nodes.description: Describe events as subsets of a sample space (the set of outcomes) using characteristics (or categories) of the outcomes, or as unions, intersections, or complements of other events ("or," "and," "not").
nodes.subdomain: Conditional Probability and the Rules of Probability

academic_standards.description: Describe events as subsets of a sample space (the set of outcomes) using characteristics (or categories) of the outcomes, or as unions, intersections, or complements of other events ("or," "and," "not").

nodes.title: Evaluate reports based on data
nodes.description: Evaluate reports based on data.

academic_standards.description: Evaluate reports based on data.

nodes.title: Use data from a randomized experiment to compare two treatments
nodes.description: Use data from a randomized experiment to compare two treatments; use simulations to decide if differences between parameters are significant.

academic_standards.description: Use data from a randomized experiment to compare two treatments; use simulations to decide if differences between parameters are significant.

nodes.title: Use data from a sample survey to estimate a population mean or proportion
nodes.description: Use data from a sample survey to estimate a population mean or proportion; develop a margin of error through the use of simulation models for random sampling.

academic_standards.description: Use data from a sample survey to estimate a population mean or proportion; develop a margin of error through the use of simulation models for random sampling.

nodes.title: Recognize the purposes of and differences among sample surveys, experiments, ...
nodes.description: Recognize the purposes of and differences among sample surveys, experiments, and observational studies; explain how randomization relates to each.

academic_standards.description: Recognize the purposes of and differences among sample surveys, experiments, and observational studies; explain how randomization relates to each.

nodes.title: Decide if a specified model is consistent with results from a given data-gene...
nodes.description: Decide if a specified model is consistent with results from a given data-generating process, e.g., using simulation.

academic_standards.description: Decide if a specified model is consistent with results from a given data-generating process, e.g., using simulation.

nodes.title: Understand statistics as a process for making inferences about population par...
nodes.description: Understand statistics as a process for making inferences about population parameters based on a random sample from that population.

academic_standards.description: Understand statistics as a process for making inferences about population parameters based on a random sample from that population.

nodes.title: Distinguish between correlation and causation
nodes.description: Distinguish between correlation and causation.

academic_standards.description: Distinguish between correlation and causation.

nodes.title: Compute (using technology) and interpret the correlation coefficient of a lin...
nodes.description: Compute (using technology) and interpret the correlation coefficient of a linear fit.

academic_standards.description: Compute (using technology) and interpret the correlation coefficient of a linear fit.

nodes.title: Interpret the slope (rate of change) and the intercept (constant term) of a l...
nodes.description: Interpret the slope (rate of change) and the intercept (constant term) of a linear model in the context of the data.

academic_standards.description: Interpret the slope (rate of change) and the intercept (constant term) of a linear model in the context of the data.

nodes.title: Represent data on two quantitative variables on a scatter plot, and describe ...
nodes.description: Represent data on two quantitative variables on a scatter plot, and describe how the variables are related.

academic_standards.description: Represent data on two quantitative variables on a scatter plot, and describe how the variables are related.

nodes.title: Summarize categorical data for two categories in two-way frequency tables
nodes.description: Summarize categorical data for two categories in two-way frequency tables. Interpret relative frequencies in the context of the data (including joint, marginal, and conditional relative frequencies). Recognize possible associations and trends in the data.

academic_standards.description: Summarize categorical data for two categories in two-way frequency tables. Interpret relative frequencies in the context of the data (including joint, marginal, and conditional relative frequencies). Recognize possible associations and trends in the data.

nodes.title: Use the mean and standard deviation of a data set to fit it to a normal distr...
nodes.description: Use the mean and standard deviation of a data set to fit it to a normal distribution and to estimate population percentages. Recognize that there are data sets for which such a procedure is not appropriate. Use calculators, spreadsheets, and tables to estimate areas under the normal curve.

academic_standards.description: Use the mean and standard deviation of a data set to fit it to a normal distribution and to estimate population percentages. Recognize that there are data sets for which such a procedure is not appropriate. Use calculators, spreadsheets, and tables to estimate areas under the normal curve.

nodes.title: Interpret differences in shape, center, and spread in the context of the data...
nodes.description: Interpret differences in shape, center, and spread in the context of the data sets, accounting for possible effects of extreme data points (outliers).

academic_standards.description: Interpret differences in shape, center, and spread in the context of the data sets, accounting for possible effects of extreme data points (outliers).

nodes.title: Use statistics appropriate to the shape of the data distribution to compare c...
nodes.description: Use statistics appropriate to the shape of the data distribution to compare center (median, mean) and spread (interquartile range, standard deviation) of two or more different data sets.

academic_standards.description: Use statistics appropriate to the shape of the data distribution to compare center (median, mean) and spread (interquartile range, standard deviation) of two or more different data sets.

nodes.title: Represent data with plots on the real number line (dot plots, histograms, and...
nodes.description: Represent data with plots on the real number line (dot plots, histograms, and box plots).

academic_standards.description: Represent data with plots on the real number line (dot plots, histograms, and box plots).
